moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% and QQQ to 15% to reduce overall exposure and raise cash.
Buy ANET at 5% to add a high-quality name with a strong scanner setup.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
Maintain a significant cash buffer after these adjustments.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% monthly, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect lower volatility, but also more modest returns.

moves.note: Reduce leveraged ETF exposure to small
Add quality tech with strong setup

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL to eliminate the underperforming leveraged ETF.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% to address its downtrend and raise cash.
Add to CRWD to 15% to lean into an uptrending AI/Software name.
Buy ANET at 5% to add a quality tech name with a strong scanner setup.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% monthly, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index and quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and growth potential.

moves.note: Add quality tech with strong setup

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL to eliminate the underperforming leveraged ETF.
Trim QQQI to 10% to reduce exposure to the downtrending income ETF.
Add to CRWD to 20% to lean into its strong uptrend and AI/Software theme.
Buy NOW at 10% as the highest-scoring scanner setup for new exposure.
Buy ANET at 10% to add another strong-scoring quality tech name.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly, aiming for stretched growth. It utilizes leverage more strategically and concentrates in high-scoring setups. Be aware that this approach significantly increases both upside potential and drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs can decay daily.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell CRWD and SMH to reduce high-beta single name and sector exposure.
Add to QQQI to 30% to increase exposure to income-generating ETFs.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and future opportunities.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% monthly, focusing on parking cash for yield. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta names, rotating into income and broad index ETFs. While aiming for steady distributions, remember these are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
